stories.headline: US and Iran still pursuing interim deal over frozen funds despite exchange of strikes, sources tell Reuters
stories.summary: Three Iranian sources and a European official told Reuters that, even amid the renewed strikes, Washington and Tehran are still exchanging messages over a memorandum of understanding, including a mechanism to release tens of billions of dollars in frozen Iranian funds. They said a political understanding had been reached but that some details remained unresolved.

perspectives.keyFraming: Military escalation and back-channel diplomacy are running in parallel; an interim deal may still be within reach.
perspectives.summary: Per Reuters reporting carried by Rappler, Iran and the US kept exchanging messages over a memorandum of understanding even as they struck each other; the sources said a political understanding had been reached, with a mechanism for releasing frozen Iranian funds among the remaining sticking points.

sources.title: US and Iran wrangle over frozen funds as they edge toward interim deal, Iranian sources say

perspectives.keyFraming: Trump frames Iran as having stalled and warns it will 'pay the price,' while signaling a deal is still possible on US terms.
perspectives.summary: CBS News reported Trump saying Iran had "taken too long to negotiate a deal" and would "pay the price," pledging to hit Iran hard even as US officials indicated negotiations remained alive.

sources.title: Live updates: U.S. and Iran trade attacks after Trump pledges Tehran will 'pay the price'

stories.headline: Zelensky to attend G7 in France as Europe presses Trump to back new Russia talks; Kremlin rejects EU role
stories.summary: France confirmed President Zelensky will attend the G7 summit in France on June 15-17, where European leaders plan to press President Trump to back renewed negotiations with Russia. The E3 and Ukraine have demanded an immediate ceasefire from the current line of contact plus binding security guarantees, while the Kremlin says US mediation is 'on hold' and rejects any EU role as Russian strikes intensify ahead of the summit.

perspectives.keyFraming: Europe seeks Trump's backing to revive talks and rebuild G7 consensus on Ukraine.
perspectives.summary: Coverage reported Macron calling Zelensky's G7 presence key to 'rebuild consensus,' with the E3's ceasefire-from-current-line terms anchoring the European position. Kyiv is pressing for more Patriot interceptors and tougher Russia sanctions as Russian attacks intensify in the run-up to the June 15-17 summit.

sources.title: Zelensky plans to attend G7 summit in France, Politico reports
Zelensky Expected to Attend G7 Summit in France as Russian Attacks Intensify

stories.headline: EU leaders back two-year Ukraine funding and advance a loan secured by frozen Russian assets; 21st sanctions package presented
stories.summary: European Council leaders agreed to fund Ukraine through 2026-27 and to advance a G7 loan repaid from proceeds of immobilized Russian assets, alongside a proposed 21st sanctions package targeting Russian energy, finance and trade. The use of frozen Russian assets remained the most contested element among member states as US support wavered.

perspectives.keyFraming: Wire coverage frames an unprecedented EU step to backstop Ukraine as US backing wavers.
perspectives.summary: AP reported EU leaders agreed to fund Ukraine for two years and to move ahead with disbursing a loan repaid from the proceeds of frozen Russian assets, a step several capitals had resisted on legal grounds. Leaders also weighed a 21st package of sanctions on Moscow.

sources.title: European leaders agree to fund Ukraine for 2 years, advance loan backed by Russian assets

perspectives.keyFraming: Emphasizes the political stakes and divisions over tapping immobilized Russian assets.
perspectives.summary: PBS NewsHour framed the summit as a test of European resolve, with leaders preparing unprecedented steps to sustain Ukraine while wrestling with the legal and financial risks of using frozen Russian central-bank assets as collateral.

sources.title: At high-stakes summit, EU leaders prepare to take unprecedented steps to help Ukraine

stories.headline: Hamas accuses Israel of shifting Gaza's 'yellow line' westward to derail Cairo ceasefire talks as strikes continue
stories.summary: Hamas said Israel's continued westward movement of the Gaza 'yellow line' — the withdrawal boundary set under the first phase of the US ceasefire plan — and its expansion of control over Palestinian land aim to 'blow up the negotiation track' as factions and mediators met in Cairo. Israeli strikes killed several people in Gaza on June 12, with reports the army had pushed the line about 300 metres west in several areas over the past week.

perspectives.keyFraming: Hamas frames the line-shifting as a deliberate Israeli effort to sabotage a fragile US-brokered ceasefire.
perspectives.summary: The Express Tribune reported Hamas accusing Israel of moving the 'yellow line' westward and seizing more land to 'blow up the negotiation track' as talks in Cairo with Egyptian, Qatari and Turkish mediators sought to advance the next phase of the ceasefire.

sources.title: Israel expanding Gaza 'yellow line' to derail ceasefire talks, says Hamas

perspectives.keyFraming: Mediators struggle to consolidate a fragile truce as Israeli strikes keep killing Palestinians.
perspectives.summary: CBC reported that an Israeli drone strike killed people in Gaza as the ceasefire standoff continued, with mediators in Cairo trying to move to the next phase of the US plan amid disputes over Israeli troop positions and the pace of withdrawal.

sources.title: Israeli drone strike kills 2 people in Gaza as ceasefire standoff continues

stories.headline: China runs maritime law-enforcement operation east of Taiwan as KMT chair wraps US visit
stories.summary: China's Ministry of Transport conducted a 'special maritime law enforcement operation' in waters east of Taiwan to assert Beijing's sovereignty claims, the latest in a series of grey-zone pressure moves around the island. The action coincided with the end of a US visit by Kuomintang chair Cheng Li-wun, who echoed some of Beijing's positions, as Taiwan heads toward November local elections and Beijing keeps up military and law-enforcement patrols.

perspectives.keyFraming: Analysts read the operation as another incremental grey-zone step to normalize Chinese coast-guard presence east of Taiwan.
perspectives.summary: An American Enterprise Institute China-Taiwan brief described Beijing's maritime law-enforcement operation east of Taiwan as part of a pattern of normalizing patrols around the island, set against KMT chair Cheng Li-wun's US visit and the run-up to November local elections.

perspectives.keyFraming: Beijing frames its activity around Taiwan as lawful exercise of sovereignty and rejects outside interference.
perspectives.summary: At the Chinese foreign ministry's June 12 press conference, spokesperson Lin Jian reiterated that Taiwan is part of China and that Beijing's actions are an internal matter, rejecting US and Taiwanese characterizations of the patrols as coercive.

sources.outlet: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China
sources.title: Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on June 12, 2026

stories.headline: Zelensky to press Trump on new Russia sanctions at G7 in France as Iran and Ukraine dominate the agenda
stories.summary: Ahead of the G7 leaders' summit in France (June 15-17), Zelensky said militarization of Ukraine, long-range strikes and new Russia sanctions will top his agenda, and that he hopes to clarify with Trump how close Washington is to acting — noting the final decision rests with Trump. European diplomats view the summit as a chance to convince Trump that US deal proposals favour Moscow too heavily, while a possible US-Iran signing near the summit also looms large.

perspectives.keyFraming: With Russia-Ukraine talks stalled, Zelensky seeks coordinated sanctions and clarity from Trump.
perspectives.summary: Ukrainska Pravda (via Yahoo) reported Zelensky will ask Trump about introducing new sanctions against Russia at the G7, saying he wants to understand how close the US is to acting while acknowledging the decision is Trump's alone, with long-range strikes and Ukraine's defence industry also on his agenda.

sources.title: Zelenskyy to ask Trump about introducing new sanctions against Russia at G7 summit

perspectives.keyFraming: Europe aims to pull Trump back from a deal it sees as too friendly to Moscow, with Iran also overshadowing the summit.
perspectives.summary: The Times of Israel reported that Iran and Ukraine loom over the G7, with France hoping Trump will at least stay for the summit's duration and European leaders seeking to harden the line on Russia while a possible US-Iran signing nearby competes for attention.

sources.title: Iran and Ukraine loom over G7, as France hopes Trump will at least stay for duration
